textPlain: Forgotten? No mention of why we should think they are forgotten outside the headline.

Not forgotten, but this article did not mention my favourite and the most moving RFC: 2468https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc2468There's quiet genius in that choice of number by the way. 2, 4, 6, 8, who do we appreciate?Related: https://www.internetsociety.org/grants-and-awards/postel-ser...
Aren’t all PEPs, TC39s, and BIPs forms of RFCs?
These are the RFCs we know, and many others we don’t. The ones “lost” to obscurity generally deserve the fate but I enjoy reading them for the historical context. Fascinating stuff.
"With sufficient thrust, pigs fly just fine. However, this is not necessarily a good idea."
